discussion: This benchmark compares a single-shot ReAct-style actor against a Reflexion loop that retries after structured feedback. The structured evaluator produces machine-parseable scores, reasons, missing evidence, and failure modes, which makes the retry policy deterministic and report-friendly. Reflection memory is injected only after failed attempts, so the actor can revise its next answer using concrete lessons instead of vague self-critique. The tradeoff is higher latency and token cost, but the end-to-end loop is now ready for a real LLM endpoint and real Hotpot-style examples.
